highlights.text: I wrote that price in the context of a downtrend typically does two things, it “goes elevator down , then it short squeezes .” We certainly saw that tendency this week.
My edge is to trade the latter: The Short Squeezes, and all short squeezes are caused by my core setup, the Failed Breakdown.
On Tuesday a big “elevator down” sell, flushing from 5300 to 4875 at 1:30AM Wednesday morning.
With a squeeze yesterday though, that would mean “elevator down” was likely to start today.

I wrote yesterday: “I will warn now, there will be a deep elevator down sell after this squeeze that retraces a massive chunk of it.” We got this today, seeing a good flush back down to 5155 major support.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into some of massive Failed Breakdowns we’ve had this week (one Monday to squeeze 300+ points in minutes and one yesterday to squeeze 600+ points in hours).

post_title: Yesterdays Monster SPX Squeeze Was Sold, But Will The Weekly Low Hold? April 11 Plan
